title.en: APPLYING CLOSURE MEMBERS TO BOTTLES JARS, OR SIMILAR CONTAINERS; OPENING CLOSED CONTAINERS (opening or closing devices attached to, or incorporated in, containers or container closures B65D)
Closing bottles, jars or similar containers by applying stoppers (stoppers per se B65D39/00)
{Feeding stoppers (in general B65G)}
Pretreatment of stoppers, e.g. cleaning, steaming, heating, impregnating or coating; Applying resilient rings to stoppers (mechanical working of cork B27J5/00)
by inserting threadless stoppers, e.g. corks
by inserting and rotating screw stoppers
Securing stoppers, e.g. swing stoppers, which are held in position by associated pressure-applying means coacting with the bottle neck
by inserting disc closures
Closing bottles, jars or similar containers by applying caps (caps per se B65D41/00)
{Pretreatment of caps, e.g. cleaning, steaming, heating or sterilizing}
{Applying date marks, code marks, or the like, to caps during capping (applying marks to labels B65C9/46)}
by applying flanged caps, e.g. crown caps, and securing by deformation of flanges
by applying and rotating preformed threaded caps (forming threads in situ by resilient deforming means B67B3/16, by rotary capping heads B67B3/18)
by applying snap-on caps
Special measures for applying and securing caps under vacuum
Applications of control, warning, or safety devices in capping machinery
Mechanisms for causing relative movement between bottle or jar and capping head
Applying protective or decorative covers to closures; Devices for securing bottle closures with wire (infant feeding teats with means for fastening to bottles A61J11/04)
Applying protective or decorative covers to closures, e.g. by forming in situ
Devices for securing bottle closures with wire (B67B1/08 takes precedence)
Closing bottles, jars or similar containers by applying closure members, not provided for in groups B67B1/00 - B67B5/00
Hand- or power-operated devices for opening closed containers (nail pullers or extractors B25C11/00; attached to, or incorporated in, containers or container closures {B65D75/58, B65D77/30})
for removing stoppers
for removing disc-closures
for removing tightly-fitting lids or covers, e.g. of shoe-polish tins, by gripping and rotating
for removing flanged caps, e.g. crown caps
for removing threaded caps (B67B7/14 takes precedence; wrenches B25B13/00)
for breaking vacuum seals between lids or covers and bodies of preserving jars, e.g. by wedge action
{Hole-piercing devices}
{Hand-operated cutting devices}
{Power-operated cutting devices (machines for domestic use with a plurality of interchangeable units A47J43/06, A47J44/00)}
Devices for engaging tags, strips, or tongues for opening by tearing, e.g. slotted keys for opening sardine tins
Devices for removing barrel bungs

Combination tools, e.g. comprising cork-screws, can piercers, crowncap removers (combinations of opening devices with cutting tools B26; with devices serving other purposes, see appropriate subclasses, e.g. B25F; B43K29/00)
by breaking, e.g. for ampoules
Indexing codes relating to constructional features of closing machines
Orienting closure means
applying two closures on a container
Details of cap feeding devices
Aseptic features
Quick or easy connection means for connecting a capping head to a spindle
Details related to spray caps, i.e. caps comprising a pump and a tube for dispensing content from a container
